name: Nada Brahman
shortDescription: Nada Brahman translates to 'Sound Brahman' or 'Sound God,' representing the universe as a manifestation of sound vibration. It emphasizes the primal creative force of sound as the source of all existence.
description: Nada Brahman is a profound concept in Hindu philosophy, particularly within the traditions of Yoga, Vedanta, and Tantra. It signifies the universe as a manifestation of sound vibration, with Brahman, the ultimate reality, being expressed as Nada, or sound. This is not merely audible sound, but a subtle, primordial vibration that permeates all of existence. The concept suggests that everything, from the grossest matter to the subtlest consciousness, originates from and is sustained by this fundamental sound. It's closely related to the concept of 'Om' (Aum), considered the most sacred sound, embodying the essence of Brahman and the entirety of creation. The understanding of Nada Brahman involves recognizing the inherent sonic nature of reality and realizing the self through the practice of Nada Yoga, a form of yoga that focuses on internal sound currents and vibrations. By attuning oneself to these inner sounds, the practitioner seeks to merge with the universal sound of Brahman, achieving liberation and self-realization. Mantras, particularly bija-mantras (seed syllables), are considered potent expressions of Nada Brahman, capable of influencing and transforming consciousness. The study of Nada Brahman is therefore integral to understanding the interconnectedness of all things and the creative power of sound in shaping the cosmos. Shabda, meaning 'word' or 'sound,' is also crucial to understanding Nada Brahman as it represents the means by which knowledge and creation occur. It highlights the importance of sound not just as a physical phenomenon but as a fundamental aspect of reality itself.

sources.title: Nada Yoga: The Science, Psychology and Philosophy
sources.author: Shambhu Nath Bhattacharya
